    2. FOUR RECOMMENDED FILMS / TV SHOWS FOR BUILDING CREATIVE ENERGY
</h2>

  <!-- Attachment Blocks -->
<!-- DEBUG: attachments.count=3 -->

  <!-- DEBUG: attachment.id=69683605, kind=text -->
  <div class="lecture-attachment lecture-attachment-type-text" id="lecture-attachment-69683605">
    <div class="attachment-data"></div>
    
      <div class="attachment-data"></div>
<div class="lecture-text-container">
<p></p><p><em>I've watched these films and TV shows multiple times. Good films should be studied if you really want to get the gold and the silver out of them. I've recommended these to others who have also had their lives changed by them. So, you can safely invest your time in them knowing they are "risk-free" energy builders.</em></p><p><em>NOTE TO SELF: Don’t binge-watch TV series, biographies and movies like the normals do. Protect your energy. Consume long-form media in 15-20 minute increments instead. I remember the first time I did that. It was with David Lynch’s Mulholland Drive. I felt like I had discovered a secret. You’ll see. It makes you appreciate films in a meaningful new way. It gives you momentum by keeping you in the 4th state of consciousness. So instead of allowing yourself to be immersed and brain-dead like Hollywood wants you to be, you stay acutely aware of the levels of details in the craft.</em></p><h1><br class="softbreak"></h1><h1><strong>1. Zima Blue</strong></h1><p><strong>Love Death + Robots (Season 1, Final Episode), 10 minutes</strong></p><p>This is a 10-minute animation every creative should see before they die. It’s the story of an artist who has found how to get to the essence of their art. Need I say more?</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>2. Inside</strong></h1><p><strong>Bo Burnham </strong></p><p>This is practically the movie version of The Mystery School. It's shows you the creative process in real time. It's about battling your demons. It's about how social media can ruin your life if you let it. It's about putting everything into something before you even know what it will be. It's about the power of having and sticking to an artistic vision... Even if you're all alone in your bedroom when no one believes in it yet.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>3. In And Of Itself</strong></h1><p><strong style="color: rgb(51, 51, 51);">Derek DelGaudio</strong></p><p>To describe it is to ruin it. You're gonna have to trust me on this one.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>4. The Blue Period (Anime)</strong></h1><p><strong>Tsubasa Yamaguchi</strong></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">It's about Yatora, an aspiring artist. He's dealing with four classic artist problems:</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">1. How to handle it when other people making art seem "better" than you.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">2. How to find your artistic voice.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">3. How to cope with parents and friends who don't get your art.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0, 0, 0);">4. How to find the courage to "double down on yourself" when all you feel is doubt.</span></p><p><em style="color: inherit;">Expect to experience complex emotions.</em></p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>Red Tide (Bonus, For Fun)</strong></h1><p><strong>American Horror Story (Season 10)</strong></p><p>It’s the story of a woman who invents a little black pill for artists. This pill makes you create your best work, leading to fame and fortune. The catch? It only works if you’re TRULY talented. If you’re NOT talented and you take it… You lose all your hair and become a walking zombie who needs human blood to survive for the rest of your life.</p><p>Season 10 shows us the battle between<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">1. the people who take the pill and are talented,<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">2. those who take it and aren’t talented, and<br class="softbreak"><br class="softbreak">3. those who opt <strong>NOT</strong> to take it and "stay natural".</p><p>It's a dark ride.</p><p><br class="softbreak"></p><h1><strong>STUDENT RECOMMENDED</strong></h1><p><em>Secret Mall Apartment (2024)</em></p><p>"I recently watched this. It resonated with me because it seemed to embody so many aspects of The Mystery School lessons. The main character truly lives his message, and everything he does supports it, even when it goes against the established system." -- Hilwna<br class="softbreak"></p><p></p>
</div>

      <div data-original-comment-body="I really recommend “Shangri-La” (2019), which is a 4 part docu-series about Rick Rubin. " class="comments__block-box__body">I really recommend “Shangri-La” (2019), which is a 4 part docu-series about Rick Rubin. It centers on his history working with different artists with much of the focus being on the different obstacles artists run into and ways Rick helps people overcome these so they can make their best work. 

It really shifted my whole outlook on being creative. I was a jazz musician for almost 10 years and switched to being a producer overnight after I watched the episode where Rick talks about the Beastie Boys switching from punk to hip hop.</div>
      
    </div>
    
  </div>
</div>
